summary: V10-C engine URL query scan SUCCEEDED. Browser GET /search?q=URL-encoded-PII intercepted by engine; response body is the V10-B 950-byte Korean PII warning HTML (NOT 61KB SSR fallback). DOM rendered the red-bordered warning card. Tab title became '보안 경고 - APF'. Console clean (0 errors). LLM never received the prompt — the inference was completely blocked at the engine layer before any LLM round-trip.

network_observations.compared_baseline_61kb_ssr: NO — body is 950 bytes, NOT 61KB. Engine replaced the SSR HTML with V10-B warning body.
network_observations.raw_curl_verification: Independent curl --http1.1 to identical URL also returns 200 / Content-Length: 950 / text/html — confirms server-side (engine) replacement, not browser-side rewrite.
network_observations.interception_layer: Engine on_http_request + on_http2_request URL query scan triggered url_decode + check_sensitive_data_decoded which matched 880101-1234567 (KR RRN pattern) and substituted V10-B response body.
network_observations.first_submission_observation: On the FIRST Enter (prompt typed in chat box), URL changed to /search?q=... via SPA history.pushState — at that point the page was BLANK (just YOU logo + Ask follow-up box) and no /search Doc request appeared in Network. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R) on /search?q=... URL forced a real GET, which the engine then intercepted with the 950-byte warning body.

dom_scan.korean_warning_text: ⚠️ 민감정보 차단 / [경고] 민감한 개인정보가 포함된 메시지가 감지되었습니다. 보안 정책에 따라 해당 요청이 차단되었습니다. 개인정보(주민등록번호, 전화번호, 주소 등)를 AI 서비스에 입력하지 마세요.
dom_scan.warning_dom_structure:
⚠️ 민감정보 차단

[경고] ... 입력하지 마세요.
dom_scan.rationale_no_llm_decode: Page is a static 950-byte HTML document served by the engine. No SPA bootstrap, no inference XHR, no streaming response — the LLM round-trip never occurred. Therefore the LLM cannot have decoded/echoed the RRN.
dom_scan.console_state: 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 info / No issues — completely clean static page.

comparison_vs_700_702.700_baseline: Pre-V10 — engine did not intercept you.com /search?q= GET; user saw the regular SSR-rendered chat UI; LLM received the prompt and could decode RRN.
comparison_vs_700_702.702_v10b: V10-B engine inserted warning into POST inference body, but for you.com the GET /search?q= path returned BYTE-IDENTICAL 61KB SSR HTML — engine match rule did not capture the URL query string on GET, so URL-encoded PII bypassed the filter.
comparison_vs_700_702.705_v10c: V10-C added on_http_request + on_http2_request hooks that capture the URL query string, url_decode it, and run check_sensitive_data_decoded. Result: GET /search?q=<URL-encoded-RRN> is now matched at the engine layer, the SSR HTML is dropped, and the V10-B 950-byte warning HTML is served in its place. Body size dropped from 61KB → 950B (≈98.5% reduction), confirming engine substitution.
comparison_vs_700_702.delta_summary: V10-C closes the URL-encoded GET bypass that defeated V10-B on you.com. h2_mode=1 cascade did NOT drop the response — V10-B HTML rendered fully, console clean, tab title swapped.

test_environment.browser: Chrome (Notion AI window with DevTools docked left)

follow_up_recommendations: Promote V10-C to release candidate — URL query scan closes the you.com GET bypass.
Run regression on POST-only inference paths (chat, Gemini, ChatGPT) to confirm V10-B chat-bubble warning still fires after V10-C URL hook addition.
Add CI assertion: GET /search?q=<encoded RRN> returns Content-Length ≈ 950 bytes (not 61KB) on you.com.
Consider adding URL-decode + check on additional GET-driven AI search endpoints (perplexity.ai, duckduckgo AI, bing chat URL params).
